Title: Mycoviral gene integration converts a plant pathogenic fungus into a biocontrol agent
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fungal phytotoxin required for pathogenicity and plant colonization; also reported anticancer activity via NF-κB inhibition
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Encodes major virion protein; higher ORF3 transcript abundance in virus-infected strains than in ORF3 transgenic strains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in SlHN-10 and SvHN-02
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in fungus; RT-qPCR of PKS1; HPLC-MS metabolite profiling; pathogenicity assays; biocontrol spray assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses PKS1 expression, reduces Altersolanol A accumulation, decreases pigmentation and virulence, and converts fungal strains into biocontrol agents
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants presprayed with ORF3-transgenic fungal hyphal fragments 48 h before virulent strain challenge; detached leaf inoculation at 28°C
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Impaired fungal colonization/infectivity; reduced pigmentation and virulence
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ORF3 likely acts indirectly because it lacks a DNA-binding domain typical of transcription factors; stable genome integration proposed as safer than deploying intact mycovirus
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlAV1 ORF3 alone recapitulated the virus-induced hypovirulent, low-pigment phenotype in S. lycopersici and S. vesicarium. 2. ORF3 expression suppressed fungal PKS1 expression and significantly reduced Altersolanol A accumulation. 3. Spraying ORF3-transgenic fungi enhanced host plant resistance against virulent Stemphylium strains, supporting biocontrol use.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Genome integration of ORF3 into pathogenic fungi is a potential stable biocontrol engineering strategy to attenuate phytotoxin production and protect crops

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core polyketide scaffold formation for Altersolanol A
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies core scaffold and is essential for target-product biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/core enzyme; essential for product formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Polyketide intermediate(s) leading to Altersolanol A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the core polyketide synthesis step required for Altersolanol A formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxin essential for fungal pathogenicity and colonization; also reported anticancer activity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Down-regulated in SlAV1-infected strains by RNA-seq and RT-qPCR; overexpression increased Altersolanol A accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in complementation; overexpression promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; targeted gene deletion; complementation; overexpression; HPLC-MS metabolite profiling; pathogenicity assays; exogenous Altersolanol A rescue assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Altersolanol A biosynthesis, pigmentation, and virulence; deletion abolishes metabolite accumulation and pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Detached lettuce and tomato leaf assays; exogenous Altersolanol A application
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes strong loss of virulence and pigment; overexpression enhances virulence
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as the core enzyme of Altersolanol A biosynthesis; additional unidentified enzymes are also required
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlPKS1 was specifically down-regulated in SlAV1-infected strains. 2. SlPKS1 deletion eliminated yellow-brown pigment and rendered Altersolanol A undetectable. 3. Deletion mutants lost pathogenicity, while complementation restored and overexpression enhanced Altersolanol A accumulation and virulence. 4. Exogenous Altersolanol A partially rescued lesion formation, linking SlPKS1 to toxin-mediated pathogenicity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting SlPKS1 by host-induced silencing, inhibitors, or attenuation strategies could reduce Stemphylium phytotoxin production and disease severity

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core polyketide scaffold formation for Altersolanol A
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies core scaffold and is essential for target-product biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/core enzyme; essential for product formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Polyketide intermediate(s) leading to Altersolanol A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the core polyketide synthesis step required for Altersolanol A formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxin contributing to pigmentation and virulence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Down-regulated in SlAV1-infected strains by RNA-seq and RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; targeted gene deletion; complementation; HPLC-MS metabolite profiling; pathogenicity assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Altersolanol A biosynthesis, pigmentation, and virulence; deletion abolishes metabolite accumulation and pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Detached lettuce and tomato leaf assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes loss of virulence and pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional ortholog of SlPKS1 in S. vesicarium
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SvPKS1 was down-regulated in SlAV1-infected S. vesicarium. 2. SvPKS1 deletion mutants lost yellow-brown pigment and produced no detectable Altersolanol A. 3. Complementation restored metabolite accumulation and pathogenicity, confirming SvPKS1 as essential for Altersolanol A-associated virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting SvPKS1 provides a route to suppress phytotoxin biosynthesis and reduce disease caused by S. vesicarium

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core polyketide scaffold formation for Altersolanol A
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies core scaffold and is essential for target-product biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential core enzyme in homologous pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Polyketide intermediate(s) leading to Altersolanol A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes the core polyketide synthesis step for Altersolanol A biosynthesis in A. solani
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA interference; pathogenicity assay; metabolite-related phenotype assessment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for Altersolanol A biosynthesis and pathogenicity in A. solani
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as cross-species functional conservation evidence in Results, though less deeply characterized than SlPKS1/SvPKS1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNAi of AsPKS1 impaired Altersolanol A biosynthesis. 2. AsPKS1 was vital for pathogenicity of A. solani. 3. This supports conservation of PKS1 function among Altersolanol A-producing fungi.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AsPKS1 is a potential target for controlling Alternaria early blight by suppressing toxin biosynthesis
